title: End-to-end catalog entry for pilot model A673
context: A673 is the most widely used, well-characterized Ewing model and is covered by all core sources, making it the cold-start pilot. Cataloguing it end-to-end exercises the schema, gate, resolver, and validator and proves the pipeline before scale-out. Standing guardrails bind this task: open-access sources only (no dbGaP/EGA/biobank/controlled-access); COSMIC/OncoKB are link-only; donor fields are published-only with no re-identification or external linkage; every assertion carries field-level provenance; the deliverable is de-identified research metadata, never patient data and never a clinical/therapeutic recommendation.
objective: Produce the complete, field-level-provenanced A673 catalog entry end-to-end with a committed gate artifact and reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: A673 chosen because all core sources cover it; every contributing source-block passed gate-002 (artifact committed); identity resolved across all three ID systems.

Authentication status recorded (cross-checked vs. ICLAC + Cellosaurus); disease/Oncotree, fusion (EWSR1-FLI1 with breakpoint type + evidence), donor (published-only), availability, omics-availability populated; all field-level provenanced.
Completeness score recorded (before/after; after >= 90/100); entry validates in CI.
License+privacy and Domain reviewers signed off; no NC/controlled data present; no therapeutic/clinical claim.
Real outcome reachable via self-serve path: any error found in A673's upstream records is prepared as an upstream correction (submission counts toward M0; acceptance toward M3).
resources: ICLAC Register of Misidentified Cell Lines
output: catalog/ewing-cell-line-catalog-A673.json end-to-end entry plus its committed gate artifact (dataset).
